what_it_does: A canonical, update-in-place management board that organizes the remaining post-loop work into a two-track arrangement: Track 1 (THE CONVERSATION — the memory/brain system, design-only, no building until talked through with Tim) and Track 2 (THE BUILD BOARD — workflows/beats run autonomously while the conversation happens). It enumerates Track 2's work items B1-B7, states the per-beat management protocol, and lists honest constraints.
observations: Written 2026-06-10 at Tim's direction; frames the two-track split (design conversation vs autonomous build) so the board is clear by implementation-time

Track 1 bundles the memory & brain system (GC15-deep) with the mechanisms it rides (GC14 self-injection, GC3 address-accumulation, GC13 acceptance->build lifecycle) as ONE design conversation, plus the [M] UI lane tie-in and 3 pending one-word memory rows

Track 2 lists B1 (G3 data-chains build — checks/ registry + check step kind, panel/jury step kinds, per-unit ctx, shared resolve-once, re-express registry_generation as a cascade), B2 (GC10 propose_flow gated path), B3 (rolling flow programs: transcript_mine, repo_ingest, drift_radar, pattern_cluster+memory_proposals)

B4 (consolidation marks — PROPOSE-NOT-APPLY for cross-lane docs), B5 (harness->system memory migration, batched <=5/run, gated), B6 (small bounded items including ingest path normalization, trailing-comma unit, inbox litter, standing Tim-reminders), B7 (Eval #4 after G3+GC10)

Management protocol: each loop beat does RUNNING-CHECK -> ingest finished workers -> pick B1->B7 in order (B3 fills idle) -> dispatch ONE file-disjoint workflow or build serially -> verify by USE -> commit -> record
Honest constraints: cognition.py/suite.py work is SERIAL (no parallel workers on hot engine files), consolidations are propose-not-apply, board completeness depends mostly on B1
Notes Track 1 turns PREEMPT build talk; beats hold to prep-only when Tim is in conversation
standouts: The deliberate two-track arrangement separating talked-through design work from autonomously-runnable build work, so autonomous beats never preempt the design conversation
The explicit serial constraint on hot engine files (cognition.py/suite.py) — no parallel fan-out there, the lead's direct work across beats
Cross-lane docs governed by a propose-not-apply rule (produce a consolidated draft + diff for the owning session, never rewrite shared docs in place)
concerns: Being a living update-in-place board, its accuracy depends on continuous updating; as a snapshot it may not reflect current completion state
notes: This is the lead's management substrate for the two-track post-loop arrangement — Track 1 (design conversation, no building) and Track 2 (the B1-B7 build board run autonomously via beats under a stated protocol), with honest constraints on serial engine work.
questions: What is the current completion state of B1 (the G3 data-chains build) since the board notes overall completeness depends mostly on it?
Has the Track 1 design conversation occurred, unblocking the memory/brain implementation?
Are the [M] FE lane and MESSAGES.md coordination still the active cross-session arrangement?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it states it is the management substrate for the two-track arrangement and the body provides exactly that (the two tracks, the itemized build board, the protocol, the constraints).
apparent_themes: Two-track separation of design-conversation from autonomous build
File-disjoint parallel lanes vs serial hot-file work
intention_signals: Trying to keep autonomous build work moving on clearly-buildable items while protecting the memory/brain system for a deliberate design conversation with Tim, so the board is clear (or close) by the time the design is implementation-ready.
novelty: The explicit arrangement where a design conversation track PREEMPTS the autonomous build track, with beats holding to prep-only when Tim is engaged — a coordination discipline between human-design-time and agent-build-time.
contribution: Contributes the structured remaining-work inventory and the per-beat management protocol that coordinates autonomous building against a protected design conversation, including the serial-hot-file and propose-not-apply constraints.
summary_for_embedding: A canonical update-in-place management board splitting remaining work into Track 1 (the memory/brain design conversation, no building) and Track 2 (the B1-B7 autonomous build board run via loop beats), with a per-beat protocol and honest serial/propose-not-apply constraints.
intention_for_embedding: Written to coordinate autonomous build progress on clearly-buildable items while reserving the memory/brain system for a deliberate design conversation with Tim, keeping the board clear by implementation-time.
